En: An hour?
Zh: 一个小时？

En: Two hours?
Zh: 两小时？

En: Pretty long. It's drink o'clock.
Zh: 挺久了。喝酒的时间到了。

En: Not now.
Zh: 现在不行。

En: Oh *excuse me*, do you have something better to do than lust for sweet syrupy rum and lemonade? With a twist of lemon? Maybe lime? Maybe who cares, just rum?
Zh: 哎哟，*对不起*，除了渴望朗姆酒和柠檬水那甜蜜的琼浆之外，你还有什么更重要的事要做吗？再来一点柠檬？也许是酸橘？又也许，管它呢，只要朗姆酒就好？

En: Yes, you do have something better to do. Stop drooling over that stupid rum stain and go. Before it's too late.
Zh: 是的，你还有更重要的事可以做。别再对着那滩愚蠢的朗姆酒渍流口水了，赶紧走吧。要不然就太迟了。

En: Maybe I should... lick it before I go?
Zh: 也许走之前，我应该先……舔上一口？

En: [Finish thought.]
Zh: [完成思维。]

En: Sweet lord, a whole hour! And you haven't thought about rum and lemonade in all that time? You've truly extinguished all trace of yourself.
Zh: 老天爷啊，整整——小时啊！你居然没想念朗姆酒和柠檬水吗？你还真是把自己的过去抹得干干净净啊。

En: An hour would have been bad -- two hours is *mystical*. You have truly wiped out all trace of yourself if you haven't thought about rum and lemonade yet.
Zh: 一个小时就够糟糕的了——两个小时更*玄妙*。如果你真的没想过朗姆酒和柠檬水，那你还真是完全抹去了自己的所有痕迹。

En: I've been secretly thinking about it all this time!
Zh: 我一直在背地里暗戳戳的想着它们呢！

En: Actually, *should* I be thinking about this? Looks like drinking hasn't turned out too well for me.
Zh: 事实上，我*应该*想吗？喝酒看起来对我不是太好。

En: Now that you mention it, I do *need* a rum and lemonade. Isn't that a bar over there?
Zh: 既然你都提了，我确实*需要*一杯朗姆柠檬水。这附近不是有间酒吧吗？

En: Atta boy! It's coming back to him! You had your mesolimbic reward pathway worried there. Not thinking about drinking all that time... It was like you weren't *yourself*.
Zh: 好小子！他又变回来了！你的中脑边缘奖励通路好担心呢。完全没想过喝酒……感觉好像你变得不像*你自己*了。

En: MAYBE YOU HAVEN'T TURNED OUT WELL FOR YOUR DRINKING?! Have you thought about that?! Get a god damn rum and lemonade into yourself, boy! Or better yet -- lick that stain off the counter!
Zh: 也许你的酒喝的还不够到位？！难道你没想到过这种可能吗？！赶快来上一杯朗姆柠檬水啊，伙计！或者更好的是——把柜台上的污渍舔干净！

En: There he is, the old boy the devil! You rascal, you! Lets celebrate right now by licking that stain off the counter. Do it, it'll be *wild*!
Zh: 他来了，那个老男孩，那个恶魔！你这个无赖，就是你！我们马上来庆祝吧，快把柜台上的污渍全舔干净！快点啊，这种感觉一定很*狂野*！

En: Forget about the bar... Or don't forget about it, you should totally try to get a drink there too. But FIRST you should lick that stain off the counter! Yeah, man! Lets get WILD! Lick it!
Zh: 忘了酒吧……或者，还是别忘，你完全应该去那儿弄杯喝的。不过，你应该先把柜台上的污渍舔掉！没错，伙计！狂野起来吧！快舔它！

En: Maybe later.
Zh: 以后再说吧。

En: Maybe later? What is this, casual Friday? What's with the lackadaisical lax-man attitude? You used to be *passionate* about getting your drink on. Show me you're still young. Show me you have the FIRE in you! Lick that stain off the counter RIGHT NOW.
Zh: “以后再说”？这是干嘛，休闲星期五？这种无精打采的态度是怎么回事？你以前对喝酒一直很*热情*啊。你还很年轻，快证明给我看。证明给我看，你胸中的那团火还在！就是现在，马上给我舔掉柜台上的那滩污渍。

En: I'll lick it later.
Zh: 我以后再舔。

En: I don't want to lick it.
Zh: 我不想舔。

En: RIGHT NOW MOTHERFUCKER!
Zh: 现在就舔，你个狗娘养的！

En: Lick it.
Zh: 舔。

En: Don't lick it.
Zh: 不舔。

En: Lick it, but only a little.
Zh: 舔，但就一点点。

En: What happened, man? You used to be *cool*. Go get your boring normal person drink then...
Zh: 出什么事了，兄弟？你以前多*酷*啊。赶快找个无聊的正常人喝一杯去吧……

En: You press your tongue against the counter. The stain is crusted at first, but after a couple of licks it starts to melt like a snowflake. If snowflakes were made of spiced black rum. Maybe it's your imagination but there's already some lemonade in it too. And some human hairs.
Zh: 你的舌头抵在了柜台上。那滩污渍有些陈旧，但是舔了几下之后，它开始像雪花一样融化了——如果雪花是香料黑朗姆酒做的话。也许只是你的想象，不过确实像是尝到些许柠檬的味道。还有人类的头发。

En: You could definitely go for some more. The titillating scent of alcohol in your nostrils was beautiful.
Zh: 你绝对可以再多来一点。酒精那煽情的香味萦绕在你的鼻腔，感觉实在是太美了。

En: It would go well with those cigarettes. That's a *great* combination.
Zh: 它和那些香烟应该很配。这是个*绝佳*的组合。

En: Get your drink on and your act together.
Zh: 赶快喝起来，一起行动起来吧。

En: You lick it but only a little. Only with the tip of your tongue because you're a pleasure delayer. After two or three licks the crust begins to melt and a sweetness breaks loose. The sharp titillating scent of alcohol rises to your nostrils. It's dizzying.
Zh: 你只用舌尖轻轻舔了一下，因为你是个快感延迟器。又试了两三下之后，陈旧的污渍开始融化，一股甜蜜的味道从中挣脱出来。酒精那尖锐煽情的香味萦绕在你的鼻腔，让人眼花缭乱。

En: A real drink would go well with those cigarettes. That's a *great* combination.
Zh: 要想真正喝爽，还得配上香烟。那才是*绝佳*的组合。

En: You could definitely go for some more.
Zh: 你绝对可以再多来一点。

En: Yeah man, yeah! Your mesolimbic reward pathway is going wild at the thought. Lick it, lick it, lick it your receptors chant.
Zh: 对了，兄弟，就是这样！你的中脑缘奖励回路一想到这里就快疯掉了。舔它，快舔，用你的神经末梢舔一下。

En: That sugary black rum stain on the counter makes you teary-eyed with joy. It's almost touching how syrupy and sticky it is. How long have you been up already?
Zh: 柜台上那甜蜜的黑朗姆污渍让你开心的热泪盈眶。它是那么的甜蜜，那么的粘稠，让人触动不已。你已经多久没碰过了？

En: Calmly the lieutenant looks out of the window, then to you licking the tasty rum stain off the counter, then out of the window again.
Zh: 警督平静地看着窗外，然后回头望向正在舔弄柜台上美味朗姆酒污渍的你，接着又转头看往窗外。

En: Calmly the lieutenant looks out of the window, then to you licking the tasty rum stain off the counter -- but only a *little* -- then out of the window again.
Zh: 警督平静地看着窗外，然后回头望向正在舔弄柜台上那*一点点*美味朗姆酒污渍的你，接着又转头看往窗外。

En: Investigating.
Zh: “我在调查。”

En: I'm an alcoholic. I needed to get my hit.
Zh: “我是个酒鬼。我得找点刺激。”

En: I'm sorry. I drifted off into my own thoughts and now this happened.
Zh: “抱歉，我陷入了自己的思绪，结果就发生了这种事。”

En: We should move.
Zh: “我们该走了。”

En: A real drink would go well with those cigarettes you're looking for. That's a *great* combination.
Zh: 要想真正喝爽，还得配上你正在寻找的香烟。那才是*绝佳*的组合。

En: You could definitely go for some more, the titillating scent of alcohol in your nostrils was beautiful.
Zh: 你绝对可以再多来一点。酒精那煽情的香味萦绕在你的鼻腔，感觉实在是太美了。

En: What are you doing? he asks without turning his eyes from the street outside.
Zh: “你在干什么？”他开口问道，但眼睛始终望着外面的街道。

En: What are you doing? he asks without turning his eyes from the street outside.
Zh: “你在干什么？”他开口问道，但眼睛始终望着外面的街道。

En: Now that you mention it, I do *need* a rum and lemonade. I'd love for Garte to serve me up a *beverage*.
Zh: 既然你都提了，我确实*需要*一杯朗姆柠檬水。我想让加尔特给我端上一杯*酒水*。

En: It would go well with those cigarettes. That's a *great* combination.
Zh: 它和香烟应该很配。这才是*绝佳*的组合。

En: I have no idea.
Zh: 我不知道。

En: South maybe doesn't sound like somewhere you can stay if you run out of money.
Zh: 如果你没钱了，光知道你的家“也许在南边”可不太够啊。

En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维。]

En: By the way, where *is* home? The address is coming up blank and this place sure isn't it...
Zh: 话说回来，*家*在哪里？地址那里是空白的，这个地方肯定不是……

En: As I said, I don't think I have one.
Zh: 就像我说过的，我觉得自己好像没有。

En: I really don't remember.
Zh: 我真的记不起来了。

En: But you've been at this hostel-cafeteria for only three nights. Where were you before? You had to be somewhere.
Zh: 但你只在这个旅社待了三个晚上。之前你都在哪？肯定在什么地方吧。

En: I don't know... near? South maybe.
Zh: 我不知道……附近？也许在南边。

En: Far away?
Zh: 很远？

En: Up on Marvel Hill.
Zh: 在马弗尔山上。

En: Why did you say that?
Zh: 你为什么这么说？

En: These are just synapses firing. It doesn't mean anything.
Zh: 只不过是神经突触的火花，其实没什么意义。

En: The name must have some meaning for me.
Zh: 这个名字对我来说肯定有某种意义。

En: In time or space?
Zh: 你是指时间还是空间？

En: Both.
Zh: 都有。

En: In time.
Zh: 时间。

En: You don't really know, do you?
Zh: 你真的不知道，是吧？

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi, do you know a place called Marvel Hill?
Zh: “曷城警督，你知道一个叫马弗尔山的地方吗？”

En: No... He thinks for a moment. "But isn't that an expression, not a place?"
Zh: “不知道……”他想了一会儿。“但那不是一种说辞吗，不是一个地方吧？”

En: An expression?
Zh: “一种说辞？”

En: A saying. Up on Marvel Hill -- a great, high place. One that is impossible to climb back to.
Zh: “一句谚语。一旦上了马弗尔山——那个高高在上的地方，就再也不可能回头了。”

En: The old thesaurus comes up empty. Maybe you should ask the lieutenant.
Zh: 老百科全书这里是空白的。也许你应该问问警督。

En: I don't. Does this mean I'm homeless?
Zh: 不知道。这是不是意味着我无家可归？

En: I have only a vague, blackened image.
Zh: 我只能看到一点模糊又黯淡的影像。

En: In space.
Zh: 空间。

En: Could I trace the way back somehow? To the exact street, the exact number on a building...
Zh: 我能有办法追溯过去吗？想到具体的街道，大楼具体的号码……

En: I'll live in a dumpster, I don't care. Fuck everything. Hobocop.
Zh: 爷住在垃圾堆里。爷不在乎，都给爷滚。爷是个流浪警探。

En: That doesn't sound like somewhere you can stay if you run out of money.
Zh: 如果你没钱了，那里听起来可不是你能待的地方。

En: A vague, blackened image doesn't sound like somewhere you can stay if you run out of money.
Zh: 如果你没钱了，一个模糊黯淡的影像可不像是你能待的地方。

En: You can try. Run some addresses in your head when you get the time. Maybe a street or an apartment will appear.
Zh: 你可以试试。等你有时间的时候，在你的脑子里搜索下地址。也许有一条街道或者一栋公寓会蹦出来。

En: Entertain the thought when you get the time. Maybe being a hobo *and* a cop has its advantages?
Zh: 等你有时间再稍微想想。也许做一个流浪汉*和*一个警察，也有自己的优点？

En: Do I even have one?
Zh: 我真的有家吗？

En: A vague, blackened image doesn't sound like somewhere you can stay if you run out of money.
Zh: 如果你没钱了，一个模糊黯淡的影像可不像是你能待的地方。

En: You don't really know, do you?
Zh: 你真的不知道，是吧？

En: I have only a vague, blackened image.
Zh: 我只能看到一点模糊又黯淡的影像。

En: Something produced by the random firing of synapses doesn't sound like a place you can stay if you run out of money.
Zh: 如果你没钱了，神经突触随意迸发出的火花可不像是你能待的地方。

En: I don't. Does this mean I'm homeless?
Zh: 我没有。这是不是意味着我无家可归？

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维。]

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: You should totally sing karaoke here, the first chance you get. Your emotions need to be expressed. People need to know of your vast, oceanic soul.
Zh: 你完全应该在这里唱卡拉OK，一有机会就唱。你的感情应该得到释放。大家需要知道你如同海洋一般广博的灵魂。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: No-no, don't sing the happy song, it's stupid. Sing the sad song, it's profound.
Zh: 不不，不要唱欢快的歌，太蠢了。唱悲伤的歌，这样比较深奥。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: My soul is immense.
Zh: 我的灵魂无边无际。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: Utterly. And it needs to be heard.
Zh: 绝对的。而且它应该被其他人听到。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: Whether they like it or not. Ram it up their ears, says your adrenaline gland. Violently express yourself.
Zh: 不论他们喜不喜欢。你的肾上腺体说，把它塞进他们的耳朵。激烈地表达自己。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: Through a PA system.
Zh: 通过一套广播系统。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: By other people.
Zh: 被其他人听见。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: You should sing that sad song you were listening to last night. Thought it was obvious...
Zh: 你应该唱点悲伤的歌，就是你昨晚听的那些。我觉得这挺明显的吧……

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: Who is mistakenly identified as a cop for his prominent jaw line? Yes, sounds likely. You should probably go on stage and pose for a moment when you're done with this thought. See if it works.
Zh: 谁会因为突出的下巴线条而被错认为是一个警察？是的，听起来似乎是这样。也许你想完之后，应该站到舞台上摆个姿势。看看这样能不能行。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: My soul is puny.
Zh: 我的灵魂微不足道。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: My soul is modest. It's normal sized.
Zh: 我的灵魂很谦逊。大小很普通。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: My soul's cubic content is obscured by the hangover.
Zh: 我灵魂的容积被宿醉湮没了。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: Of course. Like a small animal that only comes out at night. All it needs -- is to be heard.
Zh: 当然。就像一只在夜间活动的小动物。它唯一需要的——就是被听到。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: Exactly. It's measured, level-headed, and it needs to be heard.
Zh: 没错。它慎重，冷静，而且需要被听到。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: Of course. At this point, precise measurements of your soul can only be performed from the outside. It needs to be heard.
Zh: 当然。此时此刻，要想精准测量你的灵魂只能去外面。它需要被人们听见。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: This goes well with a theory I'm developing: that I'm a down-on-my-luck superstar person.
Zh: 这和我正在研究的一个理论很相配：我是个不走运的超级明星。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: I was thinking maybe I could sing something happy. Like, from those Ostentatious Orchestrations folks...
Zh: 我在想也许可以唱些欢快的东西。比如说，招摇乐团的曲子……

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: What should I sing, when it comes to it?
Zh: 说到这个，我该唱什么呢？

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: [Finish thought.]
Zh: [完成思维。]

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: There has been precisely no evidence to the contrary. So yes, Yes, it's likely. You should probably go on stage and pose for a moment when you're done with this thought. See if it works.
Zh: 恰恰是没有相反的证据。所以是的，没错，很可能是这样。也许你想完之后，应该站到舞台上摆个姿势。看看这样能不能行。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: You should sing the sad small church song from that tape you found. Thought it was obvious...
Zh: 你应该唱点悲伤的教堂小调，就是你找到的磁带里的那些。虽然很明显……

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: Of course! They'll really get a gauge on my soul with that one.
Zh: 当然！他们真的会用它来衡量我的灵魂。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: You have not yet stumbled on the right *lamentation*. But it's out there. It'll come to you. You'll wreak havoc with it, don't worry.
Zh: 你还没有碰到正确的*悲叹*。但是它就在那里，会朝你走来。你会跟它一起肆虐，别担心。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: You should sing the sad, slow song you were listening to last night. Thought it was obvious...
Zh: 你应该唱昨晚听的那首缓慢忧伤的歌。虽然很明显……

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: I was thinking maybe I could sing something happy. Get the crowd going.
Zh: 我在想也许可以唱点欢快的歌，让大家跟着动起来。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: You should sing the sad, small church song, duh. Thought it was obvious...
Zh: 你应该唱一首悲伤的教堂小调。虽然很明显……

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration
En: I was thinking maybe I could sing something happy. Get the people going.
Zh: 我在想也许可以唱点欢快的歌，让大家跟着动起来。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / dialogue karaoke inspiration

Zh: 这样才对嘛！用那首悲伤的教堂小调抹去他们脸上的假笑。看看现在谁还笑？没有人。

En: Feels like it might jump back to life any moment, the lights illuminating the white robed woman.
Zh: 灯光仿佛随时会复活一样，照亮了穿着白色长袍的女人。

En: Run your finger across the dust of the 'White Deora' machine.
Zh: ‘白色德奥拉’机器上铺满灰尘，你的手指在上面慢慢划过。

En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维。]

En: All these mesmerizing machines, just waiting to be plugged back in and played.
Zh: 这些迷人的机器，等待着被重新接上电源，供人玩乐。

En: What's 'White Deora'?
Zh: “白色德奥拉是什么？”

En: What about that other one -- the Franconigerian Ball. Wanna play that?
Zh: “另一个呢——弗兰考格斯小球。要不玩那个？”

En: How about we fire one of these bad boys up and play some ball?
Zh: “要不我们把其中一个坏孩子点燃，然后去玩会球？”

En: Some kind of... He looks around, thinking. "Inane pinball theme -- probably related to Messina during the Dolorian age. The *history themes* are the worst."
Zh: “某种……”他看向四周，思考着。“愚蠢的弹球机主题——可能跟德洛莉丝时代的墨西拿有关。*历史主题*是最糟糕的。”

En: The lieutenant grimaces, looking at the machines.
Zh: 警督看着机器，表情有些痛苦。

En: Deora was one of the three *Crown Cities* of the Dolorian era, on the Mundi isola -- the others being Rheasilvia and Advesperascit. This theme is all about early airships and beautiful, sad, pearl-laden women. It's quite nice actually.
Zh: 德奥拉是德洛莉丝时代蒙迪洲上最著名的三座*皇冠城市*之一——另外两座是西尔维亚和艾弗斯派瑞特。这个主题说的是早期的飞艇，还有那些美丽又悲伤的戴着珍珠的女人。其实挺不错的。

En: No.
Zh: “不用。”

En: You can't *fire* them up, they're broken. Only that one machine in the main hall works. The Royalist Pinball. He looks away.
Zh: “你不能*点燃*它们，因为已经坏掉了。只有大厅里的那台机器还在运作。那台皇家弹球机。”他看向远处。

En: What a dumb name -- Royalist Pinball. If they weren't broken, he would kick one of these machines about now.
Zh: 真是个蠢名字——皇家弹球机。如果它们没有坏，他现在就会朝着其中一台踹上去。

En: Sounds like you don't enjoy pinball, Kim.
Zh: “听起来你不是很喜欢弹球，金。”

En: No, I love it -- I love pinball. Who doesn't love pinball? Let's move on.
Zh: “不，我很喜欢——我很爱弹球。谁不喜欢弹球呢？我们还是走吧。”

En: He doesn't.
Zh: 他不喜欢。

En: Think: Kim Kitsuragi... Kim Kitsuragi...
Zh: 想想：金·曷城……金·曷城……

En: Kim "Pinball" Kitsuragi.
Zh: 金・“弹球”・曷城。

En: Kim 'Pinball' Kitsuragi!
Zh: 金·“弹球”·曷城！

En: It's strange that he doesn't like pinball. Kim here is a Seolite. His people are incredibly dextrous, and they all *love* pinball. Matter of fact...
Zh: 奇怪的是他不喜欢弹球。金是西奥人。他们都非常灵巧，而且全都*热爱*弹球。事实是……

En: Hey, didn't you guys, like, *invent* pinball?
Zh: “喂，我说，不应该是你们的人，*发明*了弹球吗？”

En: Us *guys*?
Zh: “我们*的人*？”

En: You know -- Seol guys. Massive pinball people.
Zh: “你知道的——西奥人。很多爱玩弹球的人。”

En: Seol is an *extremely* protectionist isola, inaccessible to the rest of mankind. As it has been for over a thousand years. I have no special knowledge of them, despite my heritage.
Zh: “西奥是一个*非常*保守的洲，其他人是很难接近的。因为它已经存在了快1000年。虽然传承于此，但我对他们并没有特别多的了解。”

En: No matter. This is a failure. I'm failing and I'm going to stop.
Zh: “没关系。这是个故障。我失灵了，我会停下来的。”

En: But even *I* know that they don't play pinball. They have a rigid class society, and a punitive justice system... He looks around. "We should continue with our exploration of this place. It doesn't matter."
Zh: “但是就算是*我*，也知道他们不喜欢玩弹球。他们的社会阶层十分严格，还拥有一套惩罚性的司法体制……”他看了看周围。“我们应该继续调查这个地方。这个没什么重要的。”

En: Stop.
Zh: 停下来。

En: Okay. He thinks for a moment. "I don't like pinball because I had to learn to play it for an undercover job at a pinball ring. And it's a lame, boring, and unchallenging game -- there. We can move on now."
Zh: “好吧。”他想了一会儿。“我不喜欢弹球，是因为我在一个弹球团伙做卧底的时候必须学习这个。真的是个别扭，无聊又没有挑战性的游戏——好了，我们现在可以走了吧。”

En: Super. Tip-top! This went pretty well all things considered.
Zh: 简直了。好的不得了！从各个方面考虑，事情都进展地挺顺利的。

En: So you're not all, like, pinball aces? Because I could swear I know you are.
Zh: “所以你们不都是，弹球高手？因为我敢发誓，你一定是。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Okay. He thinks for a moment. "I don't like pinball because I had to learn to play it for an undercover job at a pinball ring. And it's a lame, boring, and unchallenging game -- there. We can move on now."
Zh: “好吧。”他想了一会儿。“我不喜欢弹球，是因为我在一个弹球团伙做卧底的时候必须学习这个。真的是个别扭，无聊又没有挑战性的游戏——好了，我们现在可以走了吧。”

En: You still feel he's holding out on Seolite pinball tricks, but fine. Explore on then.
Zh: 你还是觉得他对西奥人的弹球技巧有所保留，不过没关系。以后再研究。

En: Let's move on then. [Finish thought.]
Zh: “我们走吧。”[完成思维。]

En: No. *They're* not -- let's move on. There's more to this place than pinball machines.
Zh: “不。*他们*不是——我们走吧。这里可远远不只有这些弹球机。”

En: Kim 'Pinball' Kitsuragi, AKA Kimball?
Zh: 金·“弹球”·曷城，又名金球？

En: Exactly. That's what he's known as. His reputation proceeds him!
Zh: 没错。这就是他的外号。他的名声让他获益良多！

En: You're Kim 'Pinball' Kitsuragi.
Zh: “你是金·‘弹球’·曷城。”

En: Hey, Kimball.
Zh: “你好啊，金球。”

En: I'm not gonna talk to him about that.
Zh: 我不会跟他提这个的。

En: So *now* he remembers... He looks at you in the silence of the workshop -- then takes his glasses off and cleans them.
Zh: “所以*现在*他想起来了……”他站在安静的工作室里，默默看着你——然后取下眼镜，轻轻擦拭镜片。

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Of course, of course. Just something that swam to the surface. Go on, do real police work. This isn't a pinball mystery.
Zh: 当然了，当然。只是有些东西浮上水面了。继续吧，去做警察该做的工作。这不是什么弹球谜题。

En: Fine. I'm Kim 'Pinball' Kitsuragi. he puts them back on. "AKA The Kimball. You remembered -- congratulations."
Zh: “好吧。我是金·‘弹球’·曷城。”他重新戴上眼镜。“又名金球。你记起来了——真是可喜可贺。”

En: You don't seem to really 'like' pinball.
Zh: “你看起来不是真的‘喜欢’弹球。”

En: He also really doesn't want to talk about it.
Zh: 他是真的不想谈这个。

En: No human being should. It is a game that requires no skill and a childlike affinity to flashing lights. And to *fantastique*, science fiction, and historic romance franchises.
Zh: “没有人应该。这个游戏完全没有任何技巧可言，只需要像孩子似的被闪光灯吸引就好。还有*幻想*，科幻小说，和历史性的浪漫特许权。”

En: It is *lame*.
Zh: “它真的*太差劲了。*”

En: Then why are you called Pinball?
Zh: “那为什么别人会叫你弹球？”

En: I am not *called* Pinball. It was used to taunt me -- a long time ago. *Before* I became a homicide detective. And got my lieutenancy.
Zh: “我*不叫*弹球。这是拿来讥讽我的——很久以前的事了，在我成为凶杀案警探*之前*。之后我就拿到了警督的头衔。”

En: I'm gonna call you Pinball now.
Zh: “我以后就叫你弹球了。”

En: Don't worry, I'll keep calling you Kim.
Zh: “别担心，我还是会叫你金的。”

En: You're Lieutenant Kitsuragi to me.
Zh: “对我而言，你是曷城警督。”

En: I'm gonna call you *Kimball* now.
Zh: “我以后就叫你*金球*了。”

En: Not only to you -- I am *Lieutenant Kitsuragi* to everyone. He nods. "Now -- we really need to continue our sweep of what appears to be a secret path through the Whirling."
Zh: “不仅仅是对于你——我在所有人面前都是*曷城警督*。”他点点头。“现在——我们真的要继续调查飞旋旅社的那条密道了。”

En: How did you...
Zh: “你是怎么……”

En: He puts his glasses back on. "Fine. I was a juvenile police officer -- for over 15 years. It's how I started out in the RCM. Once I had to infiltrate a pinball ring. As you do -- when you're a juvie cop."
Zh: 他重新戴上眼镜。“好吧。我曾经是负责青少年案件的警官——超过15年。我在RCM的职业生涯就是从那里开始的。有一次，我必须潜入一个弹球团伙。就像你一样——当你还是少年犯警探的时候。”

En: End of story.
Zh: “故事结束。”

En: You were a juvie cop for 15 years?
Zh: “你当了15年的少年犯警探？”

En: That time is over now. He looks at the pinball machine and breathes in. "I was already a 38 year old man. It was unbecoming. As was playing pinball."
Zh: “那段时间已经过去了。”他看着弹球机，呼了一口气。“我已经是个38岁的人了。这样真的很不得体。玩弹球机也一样。”

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi is good too. He nods. "Now -- we really need to continue our sweep of what appears to be a secret path through the Whirling."
Zh: “曷城警督也不错。”他点点头。“现在——我们真的要继续调查飞旋旅社的那条密道了。”

En: Wait, but I still can't remember anything *else*.
Zh: “等一下，但我还是想不起*其他事*。”

En: Wonderful. He *only* remembers hearing about the *pinball policeman*.
Zh: “真棒。他*只记得*听说过*弹球警察*的事。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Lame.
Zh: “没说服力。”

En: Unbelievably, he nods. "To do that I needed to become a... *pinball champion*. I trained for nine months. The job was successful and I was moved out of the juvenile wing -- to homicide."
Zh: “真是难以置信，”他点点头。“为此我必须变成一个……*弹球冠军*。我训练了整整9个月。工作很成功，我被调出了青少年罪案组——进了罪案组。”

En: It was not okay. I needed to become a... *pinball champion*. He shudders. "I trained for 9 months. The job was successful and I was moved out of the juvenile wing -- to homicide."
Zh: “没什么好的。我必须变成一个……*弹球冠军*。”他耸耸肩。我训练了整整9个月。工作很成功，我被调出了青少年犯罪组——进了罪案组。”

En: So that's why he doesn't want to talk to Cuno. Trauma-and-Stressor Disorder from being a juvie cop.
Zh: 所以这就是他不愿意跟坤诺说话的原因。当过青少年犯罪组警察的创伤和应激障碍症。

En: Wait, so *that's* why you didn't talk to Cuno.
Zh: “等等，所以这就是你不跟坤诺说话的*原因*。”

En: It's best if *you* handle the juvenile delinquents, he nods.
Zh: “最好由*你*来对付那些少年犯，”他点点头。

En: No. You're *gonna* call me Lieutenant Kitsuragi -- or on rare occasions 'Kim', because it's shorter. He nods. "Let's go."
Zh: “不行。你*必须*叫我曷城警督——或者偶尔可以叫我‘金’，因为这样更简短一些。”他点点头。“我们走吧。”

En: Any plan to call him Pinball or Kimball is immediately wiped from your neocortex, as if with some sort of mind altering device. It is simply not going to happen.
Zh: 叫他弹球或金球的计划瞬间从你的大脑皮层抹去，就像是有某种思想转换器一样。这件事不会发生了。

En: The theme on that pinball machine is a standard *royalist* theme -- used on everything from pinball cabinets to full-flavour cigarettes.
Zh: 那台弹球机的主题是标准的*保皇派*调调——从弹球陈列柜到所有口味的香烟都是。

En: What are its hallmarks?
Zh: 那有什么特点呢？

En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维。]

En: Clinging to a picture-book version of the past century, waiting for the King to come back and cast out all the profiteers and homo-sexuals. Basically, imagine a yellow plastic crown with a liquor brand emblazoned on it.
Zh: 沉醉于图画书般的上世纪图景，等待国王回来，驱逐所有的奸商和同性恋。基本上来说，你只要想象一个装饰着酒精商标的黄色塑料皇冠就行。

En: Wait! Where do I get such a crown?
Zh: 等等！我能去哪儿弄到那种皇冠？

En: The idea of a king and a century gone is pretty fascinating.
Zh: 国王和逝去的世纪，这个想法真挺吸引人的。

En: I'd rather know more about the world of today.
Zh: 我宁愿多了解些现在的世界。

En: The sentiment is called *antecentennial nostalgia* -- pining for a time before the turn of the century. It's common even now, after 50 years.
Zh: 这种情绪被称为“前世纪怀旧”——是对世纪之交之前一段时间的思念。就算50年后的今天，也挺常见的。

En: It comes free with a six-pack of Vermillon 'Roi d'Or.' The words 'ROI D'OR' are stamped into the crown's plastic.
Zh: 只要购买六瓶装的维米伦“金皇冠”酒就能免费得到它。“金皇冠”的字眼就刻在皇冠的塑料上。

En: The contemporary period stands still. The fated Carousel of Progress that doomed the royalists is itself winding down. Our time is decelerating -- into what, no one knows.
Zh: 眼下的时代停滞不前。宿命的前进旋转木马诅咒了保皇派，现在自己也逐渐变缓。我们的时代在退步——退步成什么，没有人知道。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: Okay, the man with the sunglasses and his *hypothetical Station 41*. Weird, right?
Zh: 好吧，一个戴着墨镜的男人，还有他*假想*中的41区警局。很诡异，对吧？

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: I guess it was kind of weird, yes.
Zh: 我猜是有点诡异。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: No, this whole interaction was perfectly normal.
Zh: 不，这整段交流都很寻常，完美的寻常。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: Frankly I'm way past caring or wondering. [Discard thought.]
Zh: 老实说，我已经不在乎，也不想去想了。[放弃思维。]

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: I know! Super weird.
Zh: 我知道！超级诡异。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: There's something we're missing here... something you can't put our finger on. You know what -- just ask him. I know it sounds crazy (and you'll probably get laughed at). But still...
Zh: 我们好像漏掉了什么……有些你也说不清的东西。你知道吗——要不直接问问他吧。我知道听起来很疯狂（而且你很可能会被取笑）。但是……

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: I was thinking the same thing. I should just ask him if we're from the same station.
Zh: 我也在想这个。我应该问问他，我们是不是同一个局里的。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: It's impossible. I don't want to waste my time.
Zh: 不可能。我不想浪费时间。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: Yes. Just cross it off the list. It's probably not true though.
Zh: 没错。还是从清单上划掉吧。再说也可能不是真的。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 ORB / vicquemare esprit afterthought
En: Of course. Yes. It's preposterous.
Zh: 当然。是的。这很荒谬。
